reasoning: As of early May 2025, there are less than two months until the July 1, 2025 resolution date. An amphibious ground invasion of Taiwan would require massive troop, ship, and logistical movements that would be visible to US intelligence and commercial satellites months in advance. There are no intelligence warnings or news reports as of May 2025 indicating that such a buildup is occurring. Instead, defense and intelligence officials broadly assess that Xi Jinping has directed the PLA to be capable of an invasion by 2027, not 2025. Recent military drills around Taiwan in April 2025 concluded without any ground invasion. Furthermore, there have been no localized landings of ground forces on outlying islands like Kinmen or Matsu. Given the absence of any prerequisite military buildup and the very short forecast horizon, the probability of an invasion occurring and being formally accused by the US before July 2025 is effectively at the minimum allowable threshold.
